channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden awards Medal of Honor to seven US Army soldiers
item.description: President Biden on Friday awarded the Medal of Honor to seven U.S. Army soldiers, most of whom received it posthumously for service during the Korea War, in his last ceremony as commander-in-chief awarding the country’s highest military honor.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KTLA 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida mom makes teen son turn himself in for armed robbery: deputies
item.description: A 15-year-old was arrested in connection with an attempted robbery that occurred on Dec 17 after his mother recognized him from an image posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: How filmmakers and actors in Asia and the Asian diaspora are expanding representation
item.description: It’s vastly different to work as an Asian actor in North American hubs such as Toronto, Los Angeles and New York — than in those in Asia, including Hong Kong and Taipei.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Blinken heads to Asia, Europe on last expected trip as top US diplomat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken will embark on what is expected to be his final overseas trip in office this weekend, traveling to South Korea, Japan and France. The State Department announced Friday that Blinken would visit Seoul, Tokyo and Paris beginning Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rudy Giuliani insists he's not hiding assets at contempt hearing over $148 million judgment
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani seemed to favorably impress a judge with three hours of testimony Friday at a contempt hearing as he insisted he's not hiding assets from lawyers trying to recover a $148 million judgment for two Georgia election workers. Judge Lewis J.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Golden Globes 2025: Who's nominated, hosting, presenting, the honorees, what they'll eat and more
item.description: BEVERLY HILLS, Calif. (AP) — The Golden Globes will return Sunday with major star appeal thanks to a slew of big name nominees, including Zendaya, Timothée Chalamet, Angelina Jolie, Daniel Craig, Denzel Washington, Ariana Grande and more.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In-N-Out opening new restaurants across several states in 2025 and beyond: Here's where
item.description: The burger chain, which does not franchise its restaurants, currently keeps its operations (mostly) to the Western half of the country.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas, New Orleans attacks not connected: police
item.description: Las Vegas police and federal officials released new details about Matthew Livelsberger, saying he was not connected to the deadly terrorist attack in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former employee charged with child abuse after babies found with 'unexplainable fractures' at Henrico Doctors' Hospital’s NICU
item.description: A former employee of Henrico Doctors' Hospital’s neonatal intensive care unit (NICU) has been charged with malicious wounding and child abuse after an investigation into babies being found with "unexplainable fractures" at the NICU.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, NewsNation

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star Zachery Ty Bryan jailed
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan was arrested this week on a domestic violence charge in South Carolina, online jail records show.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, Fox 5

item.title: Finalized tax credit for cleaner hydrogen gets a cautious OK from some environmental groups
item.description: The Biden administration released long-awaited final rules Friday for a tax credit that will send billions of dollars to producers of cleaner hydrogen.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly 700 Party City store leases heading to auction, including several Nevada locations
item.description: Nearly 700 Party City store leases will be heading to auction next month, according to an announcement Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Verizon to bring Super Bowl FanFest to Sofi, other NFL stadiums nationwide
item.description: The Caesars Superdome may be the home for the Super Bowl in New Orleans next month, but this year, festivities will extend far beyond the Bayou State, thanks to Verizon.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Nearly 700 Party City store leases, including San Diego area, heading to auction
item.description: Nearly 700 Party City store leases will be heading to auction next month, according to an announcement Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, Fox 5

item.title: Former security guard arrested in connection with Acadiana Mall incident
item.description: The Acadiana Mall was evacuated and searched Thursday evening after police received reports of a “possibly armed” individual in the mall.
item.dc:creator: Emily Enfinger, NewsNation

item.title: New international rail bridge 'milestone' connecting Laredo, Texas, to Mexico completed
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (Border Report) -- A new international railroad bridge connecting the South Texas border town of Laredo to Nuevo Laredo, Mexico, is complete, the company says.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled this week the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) lacked the authority to restore certain net neutrality rules last year, handing a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden administration officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Whip list: Lawmakers to watch in Friday’s Speaker vote
item.description: About a dozen lawmakers remain major question marks in Speaker Mike Johnson's (R-La.) quest to win a floor vote for the gavel Friday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans' trust in accuracy of US elections jumps after Trump's win, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A majority of Republicans say they are confident in the 2024 vote count after Donald Trump’s win, according to a new poll that finds a sharp turnaround from GOP voters’ skepticism about U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden awards Medal of Honor to seven US Army soldiers
item.description: President Biden on Friday awarded the Medal of Honor to seven U.S. Army soldiers, most of whom received it posthumously for service during the Korea War, in his last ceremony as commander-in-chief awarding the country’s highest military honor.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans terrorist, man in Las Vegas Cybertruck explosion shared more links in attacks just hours apart
item.description: Shamsud-Din Jabbar and Matthew Livelsberger had more in common than just using the same app to rent electric trucks used in New Year's explosive attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Orleans attacker had suspected bomb materials at home, reserved truck weeks ago, officials say
item.description: Law enforcement officials say the man who rammed a truck into a New Year's crowd had suspected bomb-making materials at his home and reserved the rental vehicle used in the attack weeks earlier.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Jim Mustian, Kevin Mcgill And Jack Brook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man accused of choking reporter in racially-motivated attack
item.description: "The allegations in this case are very concerning," District Court Deputy DA Victoria Fazzino said. "The people have victim-safety concerns (and) community safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI says driver in New Orleans rampage acted alone and was '100%' inspired by Islamic State group
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The Army veteran who drove a pickup truck into a crowd of New Year’s revelers in New Orleans acted alone, the FBI said Thursday, reversing its position from a day earlier that he likely worked with others in the deadly attack that officials said was inspired by the Islamic State g
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, JIM MUSTIAN, KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A spectacular meteor shower is about to peak. How and when to watch
item.description: (KTLA) - Stargazers are in for a treat as the Quadrantids Meteor Shower, one of the year's best, reaches its peak over the Northern Hemisphere, according to NASA. This annual meteor shower is unique because it originates from an asteroid, 2003 EH1, rather than a comet.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Downtown Omaha loses power for hours after a garbage truck gets stuck in a sinkhole
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A large portion of downtown Omaha lost power for several hours Thursday after a garbage truck got stuck in a sinkhole where utility lines had become exposed. The truck became stuck around 8 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cybertruck explosion marks second high-profile incident at Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: The Cybertruck fire and explosion was preceded by a 2021 bomb threat that was meant to serve as a message to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man pushed onto NYC subway tracks recovering while suspect is charged with attempted murder
item.description: A man survived being shoved onto subway tracks ahead of an incoming train in New York City on New Year's Eve and is expected to fully recover, relatives said, while the person accused of pushing him was being held without bail Thursday on attempted murder and assault charges.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts defends judicial independence, says it is under threat in several ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts issued a defense Tuesday of judicial independence, which he said is under threat from intimidation, disinformation and the prospect of public officials defying court orders. Roberts laid out his concerns in his annual report on the federal judiciary.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What we know about the New Year's Day attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Investigators now believe the driver who barreled through a crowd of New Year’s Day revelers in New Orleans acted alone and found he posted several videos on social media pledging his support for the Islamic State group, the FBI said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cruise ship stomach bug illnesses hit 12-year high: CDC
item.description: Cruise ships saw the highest number of stomach bug illnesses since 2012 with 16 outbreaks impacting a total of 1,894 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WGN 9

item.title: Sugar Bowl security to equal Super Bowl: New Orleans police
item.description: The New Orleans Police Department revealed Thursday that the annual Sugar Bowl will feature heightened security after a man drove directly into a crowd on Bourbon Street earlier this week, leaving 15 dead.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Trump to hold DC rally day before inauguration
item.description: The president-elect has not held a rally in the District of Columbia since he delivered a speech on the Ellipse on Jan. 6, 2021, which preceded a violent attack on the Capitol by supporters attempting to stop the certification of the 2020 election results.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Broccoli sold at Walmart stores in California, 19 other states could cause sickness: FDA
item.description: A fresh vegetables and salad company has issued a precautionary advisory for some washed and ready-to-eat broccoli florets distributed at Walmart stores in 20 states, including Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Harry Chandler, Navy medic who survived Japan's attack on Pearl Harbor, dies at 103
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Harry Chandler, a Navy medic who helped pull injured sailors from the oily waters of Pearl Harbor after the 1941 Japanese attack on the naval base, has died. He was 103.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Florida deputy identified as suspect in 1979 cold case, death of his wife: sheriff
item.description: A former deputy has been identified as the suspect in a 1979 cold case that left a 25-year-old store clerk dead, the Charlotte County Sheriff's Office announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9
